message:
chore(beads): sync local issue state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","title":"MetaballsFx: Determinism + Tiny-Area + No-Alloc Tests","description":"## Task
Add determinism, tiny-area, and no-allocation regression tests for MetaballsFx.

## Tests
- Determinism: fixed inputs (dims/time/params/theme/quality) produce a stable output hash.
- Tiny-area safety: 0x0/1x1/2x2 buffers do not panic.
- No-alloc proxy: repeated renders at stable size do not grow any internal buffers/capacities.
- Quality behavior: Reduced/Minimal produce output (and/or skip) as documented.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover edge sizes, determinism, and allocation behavior.
- Failures are debuggable (print the parameters that produced the failing hash).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:44.586858050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:21:43.691856666Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:21:43.691838763Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":529,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Test ideas:
- deterministic hash: render_bg on fixed params/time and hash the resulting PackedRgba buffer
- tiny area safety: w/h in {1,2} and narrow shapes
- steady-state no-alloc: call render_bg repeatedly with same buffer and assert capacity unchanged (plus optional allocator instrumentation later)

Acceptance:
- [ ] tests live alongside the fx module (unit tests)
- [ ] tests do not depend on demo showcase code
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4","title":"Extract Plasma Into ftui-extras (BackdropFx)","description":"## Goal
Extract plasma into `ftui-extras` as a reusable `BackdropFx` implementation.

## Scope
- Port the wave interference function into `crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/plasma.rs`.
- Provide theme-aware palettes/presets derived from `ThemeInputs`.
- Implement quality scaling (`FxQuality`) to reduce trig work at lower budgets.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: output bounds, determinism, tiny-area safety.
- No-alloc proxy: repeated renders at stable size do not grow buffers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Plasma is usable as a library effect (not demo-owned code).
- No per-frame allocations required by the effect.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:34:54.473044709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:05:53.860620349Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:05:53.860600151Z","close_reason":"PlasmaFx extracted to ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/plasma.rs with full BackdropFx implementation, theme-aware palettes, quality scaling, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extras","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":530,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

Plasma is currently implemented in:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs` (`PlasmaState`)

It is a multi-wave interference pattern with palette switching.

## Goal

Provide a library-grade `PlasmaFx` as a BackdropFx:
- deterministic
- fast, no allocations
- theme-aware palettes
- quality scaling to keep runtime responsive

## Key Design Choices

- Plasma does not need internal mutable state beyond time/palette; it can be pure `t -> color(x,y)`.
- Prefer a small number of theme-derived palettes rather than demo-only hardcoded palettes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Plasma moved into `ftui-extras`.
- [ ] Supports palette variants (theme-derived) without requiring demo helpers.
- [ ] Reduced/Minimal modes are cheaper (fewer sine calls / simplified function).
- [ ] Works as a backdrop behind a text widget without harming legibility.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:08Z"},{"id":531,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dependency Tweak (Parallelism)

Plasma extraction does not strictly depend on metaballs extraction once the shared API (`bd-l8x9.1` + `bd-l8x9.2`) is defined.

Action:
- Changed `bd-l8x9.4 -> bd-l8x9.3` from `blocks` to `related`.

Rationale:
- Allows metaballs and plasma to be implemented in parallel after the API/widget exist.
- Preserves the intent that both should share the same composability/perf conventions.","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:02:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","title":"PlasmaFx: Port Core Wave Function (Cell-Space)","description":"## Task
Port the core plasma wave function to cell-space rendering with explicit sampling steps.

## Scope
- Base wave equation (sum of sines) with normalized coordinates.
- Quality-controlled sampling step and palette lookup.
- Separate math from color mapping.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: wave output bounds and repeatability.
- Determinism test: fixed seed/time yields identical output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wave math stable for all buffer sizes.
- No NaNs or out-of-range color values.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:13.593473623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:06:12.196462197Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:06:12.196440767Z","close_reason":"Implementation already complete - all acceptance criteria met. Tests passing: determinism, tiny-area safety, quality tiers, wave bounds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":532,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Source reference: `PlasmaState::render()` in `visual_effects.rs`.
- Full mode currently uses ~6 trig evaluations per point.
- Reduced/Minimal modes should cut trig calls (e.g., 3 waves) and/or use cheaper approximations.

Acceptance:
- [ ] No per-frame allocations.
- [ ] Deterministic output given inputs.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:19Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","title":"PlasmaFx: Theme-Derived Palettes + Presets","description":"## Task
Provide theme-derived palettes and plasma presets tuned for legibility.

## Scope
- Palette generation from `ThemeInputs` with documented fallbacks.
- Presets (initial): Subtle, Neon, Monochrome.
- Quality scaling: Reduced/Minimal should cut trig work and/or simplify palette mapping without breaking the overall look.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: palette stop counts and bounds; deterministic output.
- Snapshot/regression tests: presets produce distinct visuals with readable overlay text when paired with a scrim.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Palettes are deterministic and theme-aware.
- Default preset is safe across light/dark themes.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:24.817434914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T14:34:15.043681613Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":533,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Palette strategy:
- Use 2-4 accent colors from ThemeInputs.
- Provide interpolation function (lerp) and multi-stop sampling.
- Keep it local to visual-fx; do not depend on text-effects APIs.

Presets:
- AuroraPlasma (cool)
- EmberPlasma (warm)
- NeonPlasma (high saturation)

Acceptance:
- [ ] Palettes look good across themes.
- [ ] Palette switching does not require allocation.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:31Z"}]}

- Provide interpolation function (lerp) and multi-stop sampling.
- Keep it local to visual-fx; do not depend on text-effects APIs.

Presets:
- AuroraPlasma (cool)
- EmberPlasma (warm)
- NeonPlasma (high saturation)

Acceptance:

","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:31Z"},{"id":561,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation progress: Added Aurora, Ember, Subtle, Monochrome palettes. Tests and benchmarks updated. Verification blocked by ftui-runtime build errors.","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:41:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","title":"PlasmaFx: Determinism + Performance/Quality Tests","description":"## Task
Add determinism and performance/quality regression tests for PlasmaFx.

## Tests
- Determinism: fixed inputs produce a stable output hash.
- Quality behavior: Reduced/Minimal measurably reduce work (at least by cutting trig calls / sampling complexity).
- No-alloc proxy: repeated renders at stable size do not grow buffers/capacities.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover determinism and the intended quality scaling behavior.
- Failures are debuggable (print dims/time/quality/preset ID and the failing hash).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:35.260426176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:47:24.663261300Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":534,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Tests:
- spatial variance: sample multiple points and ensure not all identical
- determinism: same inputs -> same output hash
- quality honoring: Full vs Reduced produces different output but same dimensions

Acceptance:
- [ ] tests are robust and do not depend on demo code
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5","title":"Demo Integration (Phased): Reuse Buffers + Adopt Extracted FX","description":"Refactor `ftui-demo-showcase` to (1) eliminate per-frame allocations in VisualEffectsScreen, (2) adopt extracted `ftui-extras` FX for metaballs/plasma, and (3) optionally keep hi-res rendering via canvas adapters.

This bead is intentionally phased to unblock perf work early:
- `bd-l8x9.5.1`: Painter/Canvas reuse (no per-frame allocations)
- `bd-l8x9.5.2`: metaballs/plasma via extracted BackdropFx (cell-space)
- `bd-l8x9.5.3` (optional): regain hi-res via canvas adapters
- `bd-l8x9.5.4`: fix CLI help text numbering so humans + scripts agree

## Acceptance Criteria
- VisualEffectsScreen is allocation-free in steady state.
- Metaballs/plasma paths in the demo are driven by `ftui-extras` (no duplicated algorithms).
- No behavior regressions in input handling or transitions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:47.128608152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:50.704445082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","polish","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":535,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Current Problem

`VisualEffectsScreen::view()` currently does:
- `let mut painter = Painter::for_area(...)` each frame

This allocates (pixels + colors vectors) every frame and undermines "insanely high performance".

## Refactor Goals

- Metaballs and Plasma should call the extracted `ftui-extras` code (no duplicated algorithms).
- Avoid per-frame Painter allocation by storing painter(s) inside screen state and resizing only on terminal resize.
- Keep existing transitions/controls.

## Implementation Notes

Approach A (preferred):
- Extract effect *core* so it can render both:
  - cell-space (BackdropFx)
  - painter-space (Canvas adapter)
- Demo uses painter-space to keep the wow factor.

Approach B (acceptable interim):
- Use Backdrop-only rendering for metaballs/plasma in the demo (lower-res but still good), while other effects keep using painter.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No per-frame Painter allocations in steady state.
- [ ] Metaballs and Plasma demo visuals are driven by library code.
- [ ] No behavior regressions in input/transition handling.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:36:14Z"},{"id":536,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Plan Update: Split Into Phases (Unblock Perf Work)

This issue was previously blocked on `bd-l8x9.3`/`.4` (extracted effects) and `bd-l8x9.6` (canvas adapters).

That made the demo perf fix unnecessarily wait on library extraction.

New structure:
- Phase 1: stop per-frame allocations in `VisualEffectsScreen` (Painter reuse + resize-only growth)
- Phase 2: switch metaballs/plasma to the extracted `ftui-extras` BackdropFx implementation using **cell-space** rendering (lowest coupling)
- Phase 3 (optional): switch metaballs/plasma back to **hi-res** via canvas adapters (keeping the wow factor) once adapters exist

Each phase is tracked as its own subtask with the correct blocking dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","title":"Demo Perf: Reuse Painter Buffers (No Per-Frame Allocations)","description":"## Goal
Refactor VisualEffectsScreen to reuse `Painter`/`Canvas` buffers across frames (grow-only), eliminating steady-state allocations.

## Scope
- Cache painter buffers in screen state.
- Resize-only growth; clear on size changes.
- Keep per-effect state isolated from shared buffers.

## Tests / Validation
- Proxy allocation test: stable terminal size does not grow painter buffer capacity after warm-up.
- Unit test: resize triggers growth and clears appropriately.
- E2E: `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` should exercise the VFX screen once screen 12 is included.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No per-frame buffer growth after warm-up at a stable terminal size.
- No behavior regressions in VFX rendering/transitions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:18.387194507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:16:28.355358061Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:16:28.355333815Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":537,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Notes

Target file:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Current anti-pattern:
- `Painter::for_area(...)` per frame (allocates `pixels` + `colors` vectors every frame).

Refactor sketch:
- Store one or more `Painter` instances inside `VisualEffectsScreen` state.
- Track the last required subpixel dimensions and `Mode`.
- On render:
  - compute required subpixel dims from `area` and `Mode`
  - if dims unchanged: `painter.clear()` and reuse
  - if dims grew: reallocate once, then reuse
  - if dims shrank: keep capacity; just treat as smaller logical size

Validation:
- Run `./scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (screen navigation exercises the visual effects screen).
- Add a focused no-alloc check (unit test or debug-only counter) that asserts no `Vec` growth occurs after the first render at a stable size.

Acceptance:
- [ ] steady-state frames in VisualEffectsScreen allocate 0 bytes
- [ ] resize triggers at most one reallocation per growth event
- [ ] no behavior regressions (transitions/controls still work)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:50Z"},{"id":538,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented painter buffer reuse in VisualEffectsScreen: cached Painter in state with grow-only sizing via Painter::ensure_for_area, render via CanvasRef (no cloning), and added unit tests for stable-size reuse and grow-only resize. Added Painter::buffer_len/ensure_size helpers and CanvasRef in ftui-extras. fmt/check/clippy pass.","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:16:24Z"}]}
